revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那覇市}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、…」
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那覇市}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那覇市}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |画像 = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）

revision.comment: Created page with "Among the shrines covered in the [[Ryūkyū Shintō-ki]] are their original religious afilations. {{nihongo|Seike|聖家}} shrines affiliated with [[Shingon Buddhism]] # [[Naminoue Shrine]] ( {{ill|Gokoku-ji (Okinawa)|ja|護国寺 (那覇市)|lt=Gokoku-ji|kk=(Okinawa)}} ) # {{ill|Oki-no-gū‎|ja|沖宮}} ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺}} ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|ja|普天満宮}} ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|ja|神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji}} ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=..."
revision.text:
Among the shrines covered in the [[Ryūkyū Shintō-ki]] are their original religious afilations.
{{nihongo|Seike|聖家}} shrines affiliated with [[Shingon Buddhism]]
# [[Naminoue Shrine]] ( {{ill|Gokoku-ji (Okinawa)|ja|護国寺 (那覇市)|lt=Gokoku-ji|kk=(Okinawa)}} ) # {{ill|Oki-no-gū‎|ja|沖宮}} ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺}} ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|ja|普天満宮}} ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|ja|神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji}} ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en}} ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|ja|神徳寺}})
{{nihongo|Zenke|禅家}} shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]]
# {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en}} ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|ja|神応寺 (那覇市)}}), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en}} ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|ja|聖現寺}} ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en}} ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|ja|遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)}}) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}.
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|ja|金武宮}} which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|ja|金武観音寺}} and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}

revision.comment: added [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |画像 = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]]

堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:New pages]] to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}} [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}} [[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}} [[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}} [[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}} [[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}} [[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}} [[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}} [[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}} [[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rinkai-ji}}

堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}} [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}} [[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}} [[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}} [[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}} [[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}} [[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}} [[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}} [[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}} [[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[Category:Eight Ryukyu Temples]] [[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rinkai-ji}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}} [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}} [[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}} [[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}} [[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}} [[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}} [[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}} [[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}} [[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}} [[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Eight Ryukyu Temples]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rinkai-ji}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rinkai-ji}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）
堤防の途中の建物が臨海寺]] 創建年代は不明である。[[16世紀]]中期に築城された[[三重城]]に至る[[堤防]]の途中に位置し、[[沖宮]]の[[別当寺]]であった。[[琉球王国]]時代は[[那覇港]]に近かったこともあり、[[外国人]]の一時滞在施設として使われてきた[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555 臨海寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」。 [[明治]]以降の[[神仏分離]]により、沖宮との関係は絶たれ、両者は独自の道を歩んでいった。[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けている。戦後になり、現在地にて再建された。 == 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]天久停留所より徒歩10分。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年 {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:りんかいし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] # # ( {{ill|Rinkai-ji|en}} [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[ja:臨海寺]] ) # {{ill|Futemma-gū|en}} [[ja:普天満宮]] ( {{ill|Jingū-ji (Ginowan)|en}} [[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|lt=Jingū-ji]] ) # {{ill|10=ja|11=安里八幡宮|Asato-Hachimangū‎|8=en]] ({{ill|Shintoku-ji|en}} [[ja:神徳寺]]) {{nihongo|Zenke|禅家]] shrines affiliated with [[Rinzai school|Rinzai Zen Buddhism]] # {{ill|10=ja|11=識名宮|Shikina-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Jin'ō-ji|en}} [[ja:神応寺 (那覇市)]]), # {{ill|10=ja|11=天久宮|Ameku-gū|8=en]] ( {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}} [[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}} [[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}} [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}} [[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}} [[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}} [[ja:琉球八社]]. {{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:11]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:inkai-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Eight Ryukyu Temples]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Rinkai-ji]][[sn:Rinkai-ji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rinkai-ji}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rinkai-ji}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 臨海寺 |image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[曙 (那覇市)|曙]]1-18-19 |位置 = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 沖乃山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 不詳 |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''臨海寺'''（りんかいじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|臨海湖声（[[葛飾北斎]]画）

revision.comment: removed [[Category:Pages to be translated]]; added [[Category:Completion tier 4]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
Text replacement - "Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine" to "Ukishima Shrine and Yomochi Shrine"
Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
Text replacement - "|薬師如来|" to "|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|"
Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臨海寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|qid=Q10343918}}|prefecture={{ill|Naha|qid=Q181966}}|coordinates={{Coord|26.238583333333334|N|127.68222222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q113578587]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rinkai-ji (臨海寺) | image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] | Location = [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naha|Naha City]][[:en:Akebono|Akebono]] 1-18-19 | Coordinates = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Mountain Name = {{ill|Okinoshan|ja|沖乃山|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinoshan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Temple Name = | Doctrine = | Sect = {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|qq=|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} | Principal Image= [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] | Year of Foundation = Unknown | Founder = | Establishment = | Revival Year = | Revival Details= | Formal Name = | Alternate Name = | Pilgrimage = | Cultural Properties = | Official Website = | Official Website Name = }}
'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|qq=|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by [[:en:Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to [[:en:Mie Castle|Mie Castle]], built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|qq=|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|qq=|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]] in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

During the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]] in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臨海寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|WD=Q10343918}}|prefecture={{ill|Naha|WD=Q181966}}|coordinates={{Coord|26.238583333333334|N|127.68222222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113578587]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rinkai-ji (臨海寺) | image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] | Location = [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naha|Naha City]][[:en:Akebono|Akebono]] 1-18-19 | Coordinates = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Mountain Name = {{ill|Okinoshan|ja|沖乃山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinoshan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Temple Name = | Doctrine = | Sect = {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} | Principal Image= [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] | Year of Foundation = Unknown | Founder = | Establishment = | Revival Year = | Revival Details= | Formal Name = | Alternate Name = | Pilgrimage = | Cultural Properties = | Official Website = | Official Website Name = }}
'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by [[:en:Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to [[:en:Mie Castle|Mie Castle]], built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]] in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by [[:en:Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to [[:en:Mie Castle|Mie Castle]], built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]] in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臨海寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臨海寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臨海寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|WD=Q10343918}}|prefecture={{ill|Naha|WD=Q181966}}|coordinates={{Coord|26.238583333333334|N|127.68222222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113578587]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rinkai-ji (臨海寺) | image = [[File:Rinkai-ji.JPG|240px]] | Location = [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naha|Naha City]][[:en:Akebono|Akebono]] 1-18-19 | Coordinates = {{Coord|26|14|18.9|N|127|40|56.0|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Mountain Name = {{ill|Okinoshan|ja|沖乃山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinoshan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Temple Name = | Doctrine = | Sect = {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} | Principal Image= [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] | Year of Foundation = Unknown | Founder = | Establishment = | Revival Year = | Revival Details= | Formal Name = | Alternate Name = | Pilgrimage = | Cultural Properties = | Official Website = | Official Website Name = }}
'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by [[:en:Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to [[:en:Mie Castle|Mie Castle]], built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]] in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha City}}.
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Katsushika Hokusai}}); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to {{ill|Mie Castle|en|Mie Castle|WD=Q25100118|lt=Mie Castle}}, built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|WD=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa}} in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha City}}.
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Katsushika Hokusai}}); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to {{ill|Mie Castle|en|Mie Castle|WD=Q25100118|lt=Mie Castle}}, built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|WD=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa}} in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
== References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Publishing Office, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

'''Rinkai-ji''' (臨海寺, りんかいじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha City|1=Naha}}.
== History == [[File:琉球八景 臨海潮（湖）聲-Sound of the Lake at Rinkai (Rinkai kosei), from the series Eight Views of the Ryūkyū Islands (Ryūkyū hakkei) MET DP141125.jpg|thumb|200px|Rinkai Kosei (as depicted in a screen painting by {{ill|Hokusai|en|Hokusai|WD=Q5586|lt=Katsushika Hokusai|1=Hokusai}}); a building along the embankment]] The date of its foundation is unknown. The temple is situated along an embankment leading to {{ill|Mie Castle|en|Mie Castle|WD=Q25100118|lt=Mie Castle|1=Mie Castle}}, built in the mid-16th century, and once functioned as the bettō (administrative temple) of {{ill|Okimiya|ja|沖宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimiya|1=Oki-no-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552497}}. During the period of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, owing to its proximity to {{ill|Naha Port|ja|那覇港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Port|1=Port of Naha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642847}}, it served as a temporary lodging facility for foreigners.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/555]{{ill|Rinkai-ji|ja|臨海寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkai-ji|qq=}} Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref>
After the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}) in the Meiji period, its traditional relationship with Okimiya was severed and the two followed independent paths. The temple suffered catastrophic damage during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|WD=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa|1=Battle of Okinawa}} in 1945 (Showa 20) and was subsequently rebuilt at its current location in the postwar period.<ref name="観光"></ref>
== Access == * A 10-minute walk from the {{ill|Toshibono Bus Stop|ja|天久停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshibono Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (local bus).
